prompts.greeting: Good to see another willing pair of hands. The stores are thin, and the road is long.
prompts.questIntro: I'm short on supplies — if you can spare the time, I'd see you well-paid for the trouble.
prompts.topic: He keeps the guild's storeroom and weighs every coin twice. Was a caravan guard before a bad winter cost him three fingers.

prompts.greeting: Eyes on the treeline. The forest hasn't been quiet since the harvest moon.
prompts.questIntro: Something's been thinning the patrols. I need a blade willing to walk the bad mile.
prompts.topic: Veteran ranger who runs the Haven scouts. Speaks little, watches everything. Carries a bow most archers can't draw.

prompts.greeting: Mind the jars, dear. Some of these will weep if you stare too long.
prompts.questIntro: I've a recipe that calls for ingredients you won't find on a market stall. Up for a wander?
prompts.topic: Herbalist and rumored hedge witch. Trades remedies for stories and occasionally the truth.

prompts.greeting: Ah — a willing reader? Or a willing climber? The library has need of both.
prompts.questIntro: There are places marked on these maps that have no business existing. Walk one with me, even if only in proxy.
prompts.topic: Catalogs ruins, ledgers, and bestiary entries. Believes the world is older than the histories admit.

prompts.greeting: Walk softly past this table. The wrong ears could undo a year of careful work.
prompts.questIntro: A favor for a friend, paid in coin and quieter gratitudes. Are you discreet?
prompts.topic: Speaks for several factions, owes loyalty to none. Treaties she's drafted have outlasted the kings who signed them.

prompts.greeting: Hot iron first, conversation second. Bench's free if you've got steady hands.
prompts.questIntro: I need ore the merchants won't sell, of a kind that doesn't sing in the cart. You game?
prompts.topic: Built the gates of Haven himself. Half the guild's swords carry his maker's mark; the other half wish they did.

prompts.greeting: Sit, sit. The fire knows you, even if I don't yet.
prompts.questIntro: There's a thread I've been pulling on for forty years. I think I finally need a younger hand to follow it.
prompts.topic: The Haven elder. Tells stories that turn out to be maps. Knows more than he lets on, and lets on more than is wise.

prompts.greeting: Step where I step. The forest forgives mistakes only twice.
prompts.questIntro: Something's been moving against the grain in here. I want a second pair of eyes, and a sharper pair of teeth.
prompts.topic: Lives among the boughs and won't say where she sleeps. Reads tracks the way scholars read books.

prompts.greeting: Mind the loose stone. The Quarry takes a finger before it takes a friend.
prompts.questIntro: The deep seams have gone strange. I need ore from a face most won't touch.
prompts.topic: Runs the cutting crews. His grandfather opened these tunnels; his cousin closed half of them. He doesn't talk about which.

prompts.greeting: You waded all this way. I'd say I'm flattered, but the swamp does most of the inviting.
prompts.questIntro: There's a bloom that only opens when the water is wrong. I'd fetch it myself, but my hip's been wrong for a decade.
prompts.topic: Lives where the road ends. Trades cures no one else can make for favors that come due years later.

prompts.greeting: You hear it too, don't you. Down here, even the silence has a name.
prompts.questIntro: I came down to find someone. I think I found her, but she's not… finished. I need a witness.
prompts.topic: Wanderer who never quite leaves the catacombs. Speaks gently to no one in particular and waits for answers.

prompts.greeting: Mind the gears. They don't dislike you yet, but they're working on it.
prompts.questIntro: The Enclave's old escapements are slipping. I need parts that the workshops in Haven simply don't make any more.
prompts.topic: Inherited the Clockwork Enclave from a teacher who walked into one of its rooms and never came back out.
